summary: A superb soundboard recording (with a touch of FM fuzz) that captures an all‑time great set highlighted by a smoking Me & My Uncle and an epic Dark Star → The Other One suite.
review: Fans rave about this November 7, 1971 Harding Theatre show as one of the finest of ’71 – the band rides a wave of high energy, flawless transitions (NFA → Good Times Bad Times → NFA, Dark Star/Other One, Me & My Uncle) and even a gorgeous Comes‑a‑Time.  The SBD source is generally clear, though reviewers note occasional FM‑broadcast interference, dropouts and slight keyboard distortion; the musical performance more than outweighs those minor flaws.

ai_rating.rationale: Over a dozen enthusiastic 5‑star reviews praise the show’s performance and only cite modest, sporadic recording blemishes; the weight of musical excellence pushes the overall rating into the top tier.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Minor FM‑broadcast interference makes parts a bit fuzzy; occasional dropouts and slight keyboard distortion are mentioned, but overall the mix is clear and captures the band’s dynamics.

standout_songs: Not Fade Away → Good Times Bad Times → Not Fade Away
show_quality.setlist_flow: The concert bridges the raw 1970 vibe with the smoother 1972‑74 sound, featuring seamless jams and high‑energy transitions that keep the momentum rolling from start to finish.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His ’57 Strat is described as ‘guttural’, attacking listeners’ solar plexus – a scorching, fully exposed tone throughout.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for witty banter (the “pit stop” comment) and his steady bass foundation during the long jams.
band_member_comments.Bob: Mentioned for loquacious jokes with Jerry and Phil and overall showmanship onstage.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s early piano work is praised as a highlight, though some reviewers note occasional distortion in the keyboard mix.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann’s drumming provides the rhythmic backbone; no specific criticism was made.
